Taxonomy_x: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Spiralia; Lophotrochozoa; Annelida; Polychaeta; Sedentaria; Canalipalpata; Sabellida; Sabellidae; Perkinsiana
Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Spiralia; Lophotrochozoa; Annelida; Polychaeta; Sedentaria; Canalipalpata; Sabellida; Sabellidae; Perkinsiana
Assembly: The assembly of Perkinsiana rubra (wsPerRubr1) is based on 97X ONT data and 194X Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly process included the following steps: initial ONT assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 155 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral), totaling 1.908 Mb (with the largest being 0.328 Mb). Additionally, 5541 regions totaling 110.222 Mb (with the largest being 6.629 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. We were not able to assemble the mitochondrial genome using ptGAUL. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. During manual curation, 44 haplotypic regions were removed, totaling 137.478Mb, (with the largest being 60.15Mb). Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.

Taxonomy_x: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Chordata; Craniata; Vertebrata; Euteleostomi; Archelosauria; Archosauria; Dinosauria; Saurischia; Theropoda; Coelurosauria; Aves; Neognathae; Neoaves; Telluraves; Australaves; Passeriformes; Turdidae; Luscinia
Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Chordata; Craniata; Vertebrata; Euteleostomi; Archelosauria; Archosauria; Dinosauria; Saurischia; Theropoda; Coelurosauria; Aves; Neognathae; Neoaves; Telluraves; Australaves; Passeriformes; Turdidae; Luscinia
Assembly: This project provides the alternate haplotype genome assembly bLusSve1 of the Bluethroat. The sample was provided by Arild Johnsen, and the data generation and assembly were carried out by EBP-Nor (https://www.ebpnor.org/). bLusSve1 is based on 30x PromethION R10 Oxford Nanopore data and 55x Arima Hi-C data. The R10 ONT reads were assembled with hifiasm along with Hi-C data to generate a pair of haplotype-resolved assemblies. K-mers from the two assemblies were used to filter Hi-C reads to create two sets of reads for scaffolding the assemblies (i.e., reads used to scaffold hap1 did not contain k-mers unique to hap2). YaHS was used for scaffolding. FCS-adaptor was used to remove putative adapters, and putative contaminants were removed with FCS-GX. The chromosomes confirmed by Hi-C data are named according to size. Both sex chromosomes have been added to hap1.

Taxonomy_x: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Spiralia; Lophotrochozoa; Mollusca; Gastropoda; Caenogastropoda; Littorinimorpha; Naticoidea; Naticidae; Naticarius
Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Spiralia; Lophotrochozoa; Mollusca; Gastropoda; Caenogastropoda; Littorinimorpha; Naticoidea; Naticidae; Naticarius
Assembly: On Aug 2, 2025 this sequence version replaced OZ222379.1.; Genome assembly of Naticarius stercusmuscarum. This assembly is provided by the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly of Naticarius stercusmuscarum (xgNatSter1) is based on 34X ONT data and 104X Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly process included the following steps: initial ONT assembly generation with Hifiasm, polishing of the consensus using Medaka (with long-reads) and Hapo-G (with short-reads), removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 165 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial), totaling 5.725 Mb (with the largest being 2.391 Mb). Additionally, 2770 regions totaling 97.68 Mb (with the largest being 5.663 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using ptGAUL. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. During manual curation, no regions were tagged as allelic duplications or contaminants. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.

The assembly process included the following steps: initial ONT assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 368 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral), totaling 7.094 Mb (with the largest being 0.53 Mb). Additionally, 6214 regions totaling 129.83 Mb (with the largest being 6.632 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using ptGaul. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. During manual curation, 10 haplotypic regions were removed, totaling 12.7Mb, (with the largest being 4.4Mb). Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.

Assembly: The assembly of Didemnum vexillum (kaDidVexi2) is based on 104X ONT data and 154X Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly process included the following steps: initial ONT assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 164 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral), totaling 23.101 Mb (with the largest being 8.618 Mb). Additionally, 1611 regions totaling 55.117 Mb (with the largest being 12.259 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. During manual curation, 9 haplotypic regions and 22 contaminant sequences were removed, totaling 8.859 Mb and 1.138 Mb, respectively (with the largest being 3.657 Mb and 0.154 Mb). Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.

Assembly: The assembly of Zeugopterus regius (fZeuReg1) is based on 146X ONT data and 191X Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly process included the following steps: initial ONT assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 6 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral), totaling 0.012 Mb (with the largest being 0.003 Mb). Additionally, 613 regions totaling 9.027 Mb (with the largest being 0.453 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using ptGAUL. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. Several chromosomal regions, likely corresponding to centromeres, exhibit unusual patterns on the contact map, suggesting the presence of heterozygous variation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.

Assembly: Genome assembly of Diogenes pugilator. This assembly is provided by the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly of Diogenes pugilator (qmDioPugi1) is based on 34X ONT data and 584X Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly process included the following steps: initial ONT assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 52 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral), totaling 9.978 Mb (with the largest being 0.383 Mb). Additionally, 2216 regions totaling 340.821 Mb (with the largest being 0.816 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. During manual curation, 9 haplotypic regions, totaling 1.8Mb, (with the largest being 0.3Mb). Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.

Assembly: Genome assembly of Portunus segnis. This assembly is provided by the European Reference Genome Atlas (ERGA, https://www.erga-biodiversity.eu/) via the Biodiversity Genomics Europe project (BGE, https://biodiversitygenomics.eu/). Pacio and ONT readsets were generated. The assembly was first tested with Pacbio data, but the results proved insufficient. The strategy was therefore shifted towards assembly with ONT data. The assembly process included the following steps: initial ONT assembly generation with Flye, polishing of the consensus using Medaka (with long-reads) and Hapo-G (with short-reads), removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS.

This assembly is provided by the European Reference Genome Atlas (ERGA, https://www.erga-biodiversity.eu/) via the Biodiversity Genomics Europe project (BGE, https://biodiversitygenomics.eu/). Pacio and ONT readsets were generated. The assembly was first tested with Pacbio data, but the results proved insufficient. The strategy was therefore shifted towards assembly with ONT data. The assembly process included the following steps: initial ONT assembly generation with Flye, polishing of the consensus using Medaka (with long-reads) and Hapo-G (with short-reads), removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS.

Assembly: Primary assembly. paternal haplotig of Trio-binned genome of a male wild-type naked mole-rat. Reads in the initial assebmly were a combination of the paternal- and unassigned- reads. Assembly was completed by a combination of the vertebrate genome project and DNA-zoo projects. Mitochondrial genomes were assembled with the mitoVGP pipeline. Y-chromosome contigs were generated with a custom pipeline that levaraged male and female WGS to find strongly male-biased contigs. Male-biased contigs were re-assembled with public male NMR Hi-C data. This assembly was then upraded by employing in silico chromosome painting of the draft scaffolds in the assembly against the American Porcupine (EreDor1) assembly and against the NMR FISH-Karyoptype published in Romanenko et al., 2023 (PMID: 380307020). This upgrade corrected intrachromosomal misassemblies, placed small contigs, and placed centromeres.

Assembly: Primary assembly. Maternal haplotig of Trio-binned genome of a male wild-type naked mole-rat. Reads in the initial assebmly were a combination of the maternal- and unassigned- reads. Assembly was completed by a combination of the vertebrate genome project and DNA-zoo projects. Mitochondrial genomes were assembled with the mitoVGP pipeline. Y-chromosome contigs were generated with a custom pipeline that levaraged male and female WGS to find strongly male-biased contigs. Male-biased contigs were re-assembled with public male NMR Hi-C data. This assembly was then upraded by employing in silico chromosome painting of the draft scaffolds in the assembly against the American Porcupine (EreDor1) assembly and against the NMR FISH-Karyoptype published in Romanenko et al., 2023 (PMID: 380307020). This upgrade corrected intrachromosomal misassemblies, placed small contigs, and placed centromeres.

This upgrade corrected intrachromosomal misassemblies, placed small contigs, and placed centromeres.

Assembly: Paternal haplotig of Trio-binned genome of a male wild-type naked mole-rat. Reads in the initial assebmly were a combination of the paternal- and unassigned- reads. Assembly was completed by a combination of the vertebrate genome project and DNA-zoo projects. Mitochondrial genomes were assembled with the mitoVGP pipeline. Y-chromosome contigs were generated with a custom pipeline that levaraged male and female WGS to find strongly male-biased contigs. Male-biased contigs were re-assembled with public male NMR Hi-C data.

Assembly: The assembly of Perkinsiana rubra (wsPerRubr1) is based on 97X ONT data and 194X Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly process included the following steps: initial ONT assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 155 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral), totaling 1.908 Mb (with the largest being 0.328 Mb). Additionally, 5541 regions totaling 110.222 Mb (with the largest being 6.629 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. We were not able to assemble the mitochondrial genome using ptGAUL. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. During manual curation, 44 haplotypic regions were removed, totaling 137.478Mb, (with the largest being 60.15Mb). Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.

Assembly: The assembly of Didemnum vexillum (kaDidVexi2) is based on 104X ONT data and 154X Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly process included the following steps: initial ONT assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 164 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral), totaling 23.101 Mb (with the largest being 8.618 Mb). Additionally, 1611 regions totaling 55.117 Mb (with the largest being 12.259 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. During manual curation, 9 haplotypic regions and 22 contaminant sequences were removed, totaling 8.859 Mb and 1.138 Mb, respectively (with the largest being 3.657 Mb and 0.154 Mb). Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.

Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Chordata; Craniata; Vertebrata; Euteleostomi; Actinopterygii; Neopterygii; Teleostei; Neoteleostei; Acanthomorphata; Carangaria; Pleuronectiformes; Pleuronectoidei; Scophthalmidae; Zeugopterus
Assembly: The assembly of Zeugopterus regius (fZeuReg1) is based on 146X ONT data and 191X Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly process included the following steps: initial ONT assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 6 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral), totaling 0.012 Mb (with the largest being 0.003 Mb). Additionally, 613 regions totaling 9.027 Mb (with the largest being 0.453 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using ptGAUL. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. Several chromosomal regions, likely corresponding to centromeres, exhibit unusual patterns on the contact map, suggesting the presence of heterozygous variation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.

The assembly process included the following steps: initial ONT assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 6 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral), totaling 0.012 Mb (with the largest being 0.003 Mb). Additionally, 613 regions totaling 9.027 Mb (with the largest being 0.453 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using ptGAUL. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. Several chromosomal regions, likely corresponding to centromeres, exhibit unusual patterns on the contact map, suggesting the presence of heterozygous variation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.

Assembly: Genome assembly of Diogenes pugilator. This assembly is provided by the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly of Diogenes pugilator (qmDioPugi1) is based on 34X ONT data and 584X Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly process included the following steps: initial ONT assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 52 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral), totaling 9.978 Mb (with the largest being 0.383 Mb). Additionally, 2216 regions totaling 340.821 Mb (with the largest being 0.816 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. During manual curation, 9 haplotypic regions, totaling 1.8Mb, (with the largest being 0.3Mb). Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.

Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Ecdysozoa; Arthropoda; Crustacea; Multicrustacea; Malacostraca; Eumalacostraca; Eucarida; Decapoda; Pleocyemata; Brachyura; Eubrachyura; Portunoidea; Portunidae; Portuninae; Portunus
Assembly: Genome assembly of Portunus segnis. This assembly is provided by the European Reference Genome Atlas (ERGA, https://www.erga-biodiversity.eu/) via the Biodiversity Genomics Europe project (BGE, https://biodiversitygenomics.eu/). Pacio and ONT readsets were generated. The assembly was first tested with Pacbio data, but the results proved insufficient. The strategy was therefore shifted towards assembly with ONT data. The assembly process included the following steps: initial ONT assembly generation with Flye, polishing of the consensus using Medaka (with long-reads) and Hapo-G (with short-reads), removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS.

Assembly: Primary assembly. paternal haplotig of Trio-binned genome of a male wild-type naked mole-rat. Reads in the initial assebmly were a combination of the paternal- and unassigned- reads. Assembly was completed by a combination of the vertebrate genome project and DNA-zoo projects. Mitochondrial genomes were assembled with the mitoVGP pipeline. Y-chromosome contigs were generated with a custom pipeline that levaraged male and female WGS to find strongly male-biased contigs. Male-biased contigs were re-assembled with public male NMR Hi-C data. This assembly was then upraded by employing in silico chromosome painting of the draft scaffolds in the assembly against the American Porcupine (EreDor1) assembly and against the NMR FISH-Karyoptype published in Romanenko et al., 2023 (PMID: 380307020). This upgrade corrected intrachromosomal misassemblies, placed small contigs, and placed centromeres.

Assembly: Primary assembly. Maternal haplotig of Trio-binned genome of a male wild-type naked mole-rat. Reads in the initial assebmly were a combination of the maternal- and unassigned- reads. Assembly was completed by a combination of the vertebrate genome project and DNA-zoo projects. Mitochondrial genomes were assembled with the mitoVGP pipeline. Y-chromosome contigs were generated with a custom pipeline that levaraged male and female WGS to find strongly male-biased contigs. Male-biased contigs were re-assembled with public male NMR Hi-C data. This assembly was then upraded by employing in silico chromosome painting of the draft scaffolds in the assembly against the American Porcupine (EreDor1) assembly and against the NMR FISH-Karyoptype published in Romanenko et al., 2023 (PMID: 380307020). This upgrade corrected intrachromosomal misassemblies, placed small contigs, and placed centromeres.

Assembly was completed by a combination of the vertebrate genome project and DNA-zoo projects. Mitochondrial genomes were assembled with the mitoVGP pipeline. Y-chromosome contigs were generated with a custom pipeline that levaraged male and female WGS to find strongly male-biased contigs. Male-biased contigs were re-assembled with public male NMR Hi-C data.

Assembly: The assembly mSarHar1.11 is based on ~88x ONT data including 10x ultra long reads sequenced at Oxford Nanopore Technologies; 60x 10X Genomics Chromium data, BioNano data, ~50x Illumina HiSeq XTen and 60x Dovetail Hi-C data generated at the Wellcome Sanger Institute. The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial ONT assembly generation with WTDBG, 10X based scaffolding with scaff10x, BioNano hybrid-scaffolding, Hi-C based scaffolding with scaffHiC, WTDBG2/Racon polishing, and two rounds of FreeBayes polishing. Finally, the assembly was analysed and manually improved using gEVAL, where haplotigs have been removed. Chromosome-scale scaffolds have been confirmed using the Hi-C data. Chromosomes are named according to established convention and the labels for chromosomes 1 and 2 are switched compared with a previous genome assembly (Devil7.0) (i.e. the chromosome labelled 1 in Devil7.0 is labelled 2 in the current assembly, and vice versa). Two contigs derived from the Y chromosome from a different individual, and sequenced with Illumina HiSeq 2000, have been added. The MT sequence from Devil7.0 has also been included.

Assembly: Genome assembly of Portunus segnis. This assembly is provided by the European Reference Genome Atlas (ERGA, https://www.erga-biodiversity.eu/) via the Biodiversity Genomics Europe project (BGE, https://biodiversitygenomics.eu/). Pacio and ONT readsets were generated. The assembly was first tested with Pacbio data, but the results proved insufficient. The strategy was therefore shifted towards assembly with ONT data. The assembly process included the following steps: initial ONT assembly generation with Flye, polishing of the consensus using Medaka (with long-reads) and Hapo-G (with short-reads), removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS.

Assembly: Primary assembly. Maternal haplotig of Trio-binned genome of a male wild-type naked mole-rat. Reads in the initial assebmly were a combination of the maternal- and unassigned- reads. Assembly was completed by a combination of the vertebrate genome project and DNA-zoo projects. Mitochondrial genomes were assembled with the mitoVGP pipeline. Y-chromosome contigs were generated with a custom pipeline that levaraged male and female WGS to find strongly male-biased contigs. Male-biased contigs were re-assembled with public male NMR Hi-C data. This assembly was then upraded by employing in silico chromosome painting of the draft scaffolds in the assembly against the American Porcupine (EreDor1) assembly and against the NMR FISH-Karyoptype published in Romanenko et al., 2023 (PMID: 380307020). This upgrade corrected intrachromosomal misassemblies, placed small contigs, and placed centromeres. Ambiguous syntenic alignments were resolved by inspecting our Hi-C-seq data at the end points of those regions. Chromosome names were identified in collaboration with GeneCodehc.

Assembly: This project provides the alternate haplotype genome assembly bLusSve1 of the Bluethroat. The sample was provided by Arild Johnsen, and the data generation and assembly were carried out by EBP-Nor (https://www.ebpnor.org/). bLusSve1 is based on 30x PromethION R10 Oxford Nanopore data and 55x Arima Hi-C data. The R10 ONT reads were assembled with hifiasm along with Hi-C data to generate a pair of haplotype-resolved assemblies. K-mers from the two assemblies were used to filter Hi-C reads to create two sets of reads for scaffolding the assemblies (i.e., reads used to scaffold hap1 did not contain k-mers unique to hap2). YaHS was used for scaffolding. FCS-adaptor was used to remove putative adapters, and putative contaminants were removed with FCS-GX. The chromosomes confirmed by Hi-C data are named according to size. Both sex chromosomes have been added to hap1.

Assembly: Genome assembly of Diogenes pugilator. This assembly is provided by the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly of Diogenes pugilator (qmDioPugi1) is based on 34X ONT data and 584X Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly process included the following steps: initial ONT assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 52 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral), totaling 9.978 Mb (with the largest being 0.383 Mb). Additionally, 2216 regions totaling 340.821 Mb (with the largest being 0.816 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. During manual curation, 9 haplotypic regions, totaling 1.8Mb, (with the largest being 0.3Mb). Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.

Assembly: Genome assembly of Portunus segnis. This assembly is provided by the European Reference Genome Atlas (ERGA, https://www.erga-biodiversity.eu/) via the Biodiversity Genomics Europe project (BGE, https://biodiversitygenomics.eu/). Pacio and ONT readsets were generated. The assembly was first tested with Pacbio data, but the results proved insufficient. The strategy was therefore shifted towards assembly with ONT data. Assembly: Genome assembly of Portunus segnis. This assembly is provided by the European Reference Genome Atlas (ERGA, https://www.erga-biodiversity.eu/) via the Biodiversity Genomics Europe project (BGE, https://biodiversitygenomics.eu/). Pacio and ONT readsets were generated. The assembly was first tested with Pacbio data, but the results proved insufficient. The strategy was therefore shifted towards assembly with ONT data. The assembly process included the following steps: initial ONT assembly generation with Flye, polishing of the consensus using Medaka (with long-reads) and Hapo-G (with short-reads), removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS.

This assembly is provided by the European Reference Genome Atlas (ERGA, https://www.erga-biodiversity.eu/) via the Biodiversity Genomics Europe project (BGE, https://biodiversitygenomics.eu/). Pacio and ONT readsets were generated. The assembly was first tested with Pacbio data, but the results proved insufficient. The strategy was therefore shifted towards assembly with ONT data. The assembly process included the following steps: initial ONT assembly generation with Flye, polishing of the consensus using Medaka (with long-reads) and Hapo-G (with short-reads), removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS.

Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

Assembly: Primary assembly. Maternal haplotig of Trio-binned genome of a male wild-type naked mole-rat. Reads in the initial assebmly were a combination of the maternal- and unassigned- reads. Assembly was completed using trio-binning, the Flye aligner, and polishing, 10X-scaffolding, and Hi-C scaffolding piplines established in both the vertebrate genome project and DNA-zoo projects. Mitochondrial genomes were assembled with the mitoVGP pipeline. Y-chromosome contigs were generated with a custom pipeline that levaraged male and female WGS to find strongly male-biased contigs. Male-biased contigs were re-assembled with public male NMR Hi-C data.

Assembly: The assembly ilArcOpor1.hap1.1 is based on 51x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. This genome has been assembled using PacBio and HiC data and phased. The result is two curated haplotypes. Z chromosome identified based BUSCO gene painting with ancestral Merian elements (https://doi.org/10.1038/s41559-024-02329-4). Chromosomes Z and W were identified by copy number in the diploid assembly.The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 26 (3.6 - 4.0 Mbp) and chromosome 27 (4.9 - 5.1 Mbp) are unknown.

Assembly: Genome assembly of Diogenes pugilator. This assembly is provided by the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly of Diogenes pugilator (qmDioPugi1) is based on 34X ONT data and 584X Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly process included the following steps: initial ONT assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 52 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral), totaling 9.978 Mb (with the largest being 0.383 Mb). Additionally, 2216 regions totaling 340.821 Mb (with the largest being 0.816 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. During manual curation, 9 haplotypic regions, totaling 1.8Mb, (with the largest being 0.3Mb). Assembly: Primary assembly. Maternal haplotig of Trio-binned genome of a male wild-type naked mole-rat. Reads in the initial assebmly were a combination of the maternal- and unassigned- reads. Assembly was completed by a combination of the vertebrate genome project and DNA-zoo projects. Mitochondrial genomes were assembled with the mitoVGP pipeline. Y-chromosome contigs were generated with a custom pipeline that levaraged male and female WGS to find strongly male-biased contigs. Male-biased contigs were re-assembled with public male NMR Hi-C data. This assembly was then upraded by employing in silico chromosome painting of the draft scaffolds in the assembly against the American Porcupine (EreDor1) assembly and against the NMR FISH-Karyoptype published in Romanenko et al., 2023 (PMID: 380307020). This upgrade corrected intrachromosomal misassemblies, placed small contigs, and placed centromeres.
